extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won stage 5 sprint after Quickstep executed left-side leadout while Merlier/De Buyst on right created split peloton
tactical_patterns: Quickstep split leadout left versus Merlier right indicates deliberate two-side sprint strategy forcing positioning errors.
Schelling took golden kilometer sprint over Arts despite GC irrelevance, revealing Bora's willingness to burn matches pre-sprint.
Lotto controlled all day for Ewan yet lost indicates Quickstep's late positioning superior to sustained tempo control.

rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: negative: experienced gearing problem and headbutted De Buyst in frustration, suggesting mechanical fragility under pressure
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: won two prior stages but lost with full Lotto train support reveals vulnerability when leadout compromised
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: 151st win despite being secondary option shows peak form and ability to capitalize when positioned correctly
rider_signals.SCHELLING Ide: positive: took golden kilometer over Arts to move fifth GC demonstrates opportunistic GC awareness by non-contender
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: won overall returning from Lombardia crash indicates full recovery and time trial dominance remains intact
